title: FizzBuzz
description: Write a function fizzBuzz(n) that returns an array of strings for numbers 1 to n. For multiples of 3, use 'Fizz'; for multiples of 5, use 'Buzz'; for multiples of both, use 'FizzBuzz'; otherwise use the number as a string.
evaluationCriteria: The solution must correctly output Fizz for multiples of 3, Buzz for multiples of 5, FizzBuzz for multiples of both, and the number as a string otherwise. Check for correct loop bounds (1 to n inclusive), proper return type (array of strings), and edge cases like n=0 or n=1.

title: Reverse a String
description: Write a function reverseString(s) that returns the input string reversed. Do not use the built-in .reverse() method on an array (splitting and reversing is acceptable). Handle empty strings and single characters correctly.
evaluationCriteria: The solution must correctly reverse any string, including empty strings and palindromes. Penalize solutions that mutate the input or only work for ASCII. Reward solutions that are concise and readable. Using split+reverse+join is acceptable for MVP.

title: Find Duplicates in Array
description: Write a function findDuplicates(arr) that takes an array of integers and returns an array containing only the elements that appear more than once. The result array should have no duplicates itself. Order does not matter.
evaluationCriteria: The function must correctly identify all duplicate values. The returned array should not contain any value more than once even if it appears 3+ times in the input. Check for edge cases: empty array, array with all unique values, array where all elements are the same. A linear time solution using a frequency map is preferred over a nested loop approach.

title: Binary Search
description: Implement binarySearch(sortedArr, target) that searches for target in a sorted array of integers and returns its index, or -1 if not found. You must implement the binary search algorithm — do not use linear scan or built-in search methods.
evaluationCriteria: The implementation must use the binary search algorithm with O(log n) time complexity. Check that the mid calculation avoids integer overflow (use Math.floor((lo+hi)/2) or equivalent). Must correctly handle: empty array, single element, target at first/last position, target not present. Penalize solutions that use linear scan.

title: LRU Cache
description: Implement an LRU (Least Recently Used) Cache class with a fixed capacity. It should support two operations: get(key) — return the value if it exists, otherwise -1, and put(key, value) — insert or update the key-value pair. If the cache reaches capacity, evict the least recently used item before inserting. Both operations must run in O(1) average time.
evaluationCriteria: The LRU Cache must correctly implement get and put in O(1) time. The standard approach is a doubly linked list combined with a hash map. Verify: eviction happens when capacity is exceeded, get and put both count as 'use' for LRU purposes, capacity=1 edge case works, getting a non-existent key returns -1. Penalize solutions that use built-in OrderedDict or similar shortcuts unless the logic is correct.
